questions.question: Which of the following is a web browser?

options.option: Google Chrome
options.comment: Web browsers include Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari. Google is a search engine, not a browser.

options.option: Microsoft Word
Adobe Photoshop
Windows Explorer (File Explorer)

questions.question: What does the address bar in a browser do?

options.option: Shows and accepts the web address (URL) of the current page
options.comment: Type a URL or search term in the address bar to navigate. Ctrl+L moves focus there quickly.

options.option: Stores all your downloaded files
Shows the computer's IP address
Connects the browser to a VPN automatically

questions.question: What does Ctrl + T do in a web browser?

options.option: Opens a new tab
options.comment: Ctrl+T = new tab. Ctrl+W closes the current tab. Ctrl+N opens a new window.

options.option: Opens a new browser window
Opens the browser history
Translates the current page

questions.question: Browser bookmarks (favourites) are used to:

options.option: Save links to websites you want to revisit easily
options.comment: Bookmarks store URLs for quick access — no need to remember or retype the full address.

options.option: Remember your login passwords automatically
Download website content to your hard drive
Block unwanted pop-up advertisements

questions.question: Why does 'HTTPS' in a web address matter?

options.option: It means the connection is encrypted and more secure
options.comment: The 'S' in HTTPS stands for Secure. Avoid entering sensitive data on HTTP-only sites.

options.option: It means the website loads faster than HTTP
It confirms the website is government-approved
It means you need a password to view the page

questions.question: What is browser history used for?

options.option: To show a list of websites you have previously visited
options.comment: History (Ctrl+H) helps you find a page you visited before. You can clear it for privacy.

options.option: To show all websites on the internet
To block websites from loading again
To record your typed passwords

questions.question: What is a search engine?

options.option: A tool that finds and lists web pages matching your query
options.comment: Search engines like Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo index billions of pages and rank results by relevance.

options.option: A program that blocks harmful websites
A type of web browser for finding files
The part of the browser that translates foreign languages

questions.question: Using quotation marks around a phrase in Google, e.g. "digital literacy", will:

options.option: Return results that contain that exact phrase
options.comment: Quotes force an exact phrase match, reducing irrelevant results — a key search refinement technique.

options.option: Search only within Wikipedia articles
Find images related to the phrase only
Translate the phrase into multiple languages

questions.question: How can you tell the difference between a Google advertisement and an organic search result?

options.option: Ads are labelled 'Sponsored' and appear at the top or bottom of results
options.comment: Google labels paid results as 'Sponsored'. Organic results rank by relevance, not payment.

options.option: Ads appear only in the Images tab
Ads have a different font colour — always red
Organic results are displayed with a lock icon

questions.question: Which of these indicates a trustworthy source of information online?

options.option: An official government (.gov) or established academic (.edu) website
options.comment: Authoritative sites have clear authorship, citations, and institutional accountability.

options.option: A website that ranks first in every Google search
A website with lots of pop-up advertisements
Any website that ends in .com

questions.question: Adding 'site:' to a Google search (e.g. site:bbc.com climate) will:

options.option: Limit results to only that specific website
options.comment: The 'site:' operator is an advanced filter — handy for searching within a trusted source.

options.option: Create a shortcut to that website in your browser
Check if that website is currently online
Search all websites except that one

questions.question: When should you question information you find online?

options.option: Always — verify claims with multiple credible sources
options.comment: Anyone can publish online. Cross-referencing with authoritative sources is good digital research practice.

options.option: Only when the website looks poorly designed
Never — published content is always checked for accuracy
Only for medical and financial information

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid email address format?

options.option: username@domain.com
options.comment: An email address requires a local part, the @ symbol, and a domain name with a valid extension.

options.option: username//domain.com
www.username.domain.com
username:domain@com

questions.question: What does CC stand for in an email?

options.option: Carbon Copy — sends a visible copy to another recipient
options.comment: CC recipients are visible to all. BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) hides the extra recipients from others.

options.option: Confidential Content — marks the email private
Confirmed Contact — verifies the recipient's address
Computer Communication — a technical header field

questions.question: Where do new incoming emails appear?

options.option: The Inbox
options.comment: Inbox = new mail. Sent = sent items. Drafts = unsent. Trash/Bin = deleted. Spam = junk.

options.option: The Drafts folder
The Outbox
The Spam folder

questions.question: An email attachment is:

options.option: A file (document, image, etc.) sent alongside the email body
options.comment: Most services limit attachments to 25 MB. For larger files, share a cloud storage link instead.

options.option: A link to a website embedded in the email body
The email signature at the bottom of the message
A request for a meeting sent automatically

questions.question: What is 'Reply All' and when should you use it carefully?

options.option: It sends your reply to everyone on the thread — avoid for private replies
options.comment: Reply All is appropriate for group discussions. For personal replies, use Reply (to sender only).

options.option: It sends your email to all contacts in your address book
It schedules the reply for a later time
It marks the email as urgent for all recipients

questions.question: What is a spam email?

options.option: Unwanted bulk messages often advertising or attempting scams
options.comment: Spam clogs inboxes and can contain phishing links or malware. Never click links in spam.

options.option: An email that is too large to be delivered
An email sent to the wrong person by mistake
An email with no subject line

questions.question: Which of the following is a cloud storage service?

options.option: Google Drive
options.comment: Cloud storage services include Google Drive, Microsoft OneDrive, and Dropbox.

options.option: USB flash drive
DVD-ROM disc
The SSD inside your computer

questions.question: What is the main advantage of cloud storage over a USB flash drive?

options.option: Files can be accessed from any device with internet, and shared with others
options.comment: Cloud storage enables remote access and real-time collaboration — no physical device needed.

options.option: Cloud storage is faster to read and write than a USB drive
Cloud storage works without an internet connection
Cloud storage can only hold documents, not images

questions.question: What does 'View Only' permission mean when sharing a Google Drive file?

options.option: The recipient can read but cannot edit or comment on the file
options.comment: Drive has three sharing levels: Viewer (read only), Commenter (add notes), Editor (full changes).

options.option: The file is visible only while the owner is online
The file deletes itself after it is viewed once
The recipient must download the file to read it

questions.question: Which tool is best for collaborating on a document with colleagues in real time?

options.option: Google Docs or Microsoft 365 Online (collaborative editing)
options.comment: These tools show live edits from all collaborators simultaneously, preventing version conflicts.

options.option: Sending a .docx file as an email attachment each time
Printing the document and mailing it to each colleague
Saving the file to a USB drive and passing it around

questions.question: How do you share a file from Google Drive with someone outside your organisation?

options.option: Right-click the file, choose Share, and enter their email or generate a link
options.comment: Google Drive lets you share by email address or by generating a shareable link with chosen permissions.

options.option: Download the file and send it by post
You cannot share with people outside your organisation
Upload the file to an FTP server first

questions.question: What happens to a file on Google Drive when you delete it?

options.option: It moves to the Trash and can be restored within 30 days
options.comment: Google Drive Trash retains deleted files for 30 days before permanent deletion.

options.option: It is permanently deleted immediately from the internet
It is sent to all people you previously shared it with
It is converted to a read-only file automatically

questions.question: Which of the following is a video conferencing application?

options.option: Microsoft Teams
options.comment: Video conferencing tools include Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet.

options.option: Microsoft Excel
Adobe Reader
Google Chrome

questions.question: What should you do before joining a professional video call?

options.option: Test your microphone and camera, and ensure a quiet background
options.comment: Technical checks before a call prevent disruptions and show professionalism.

options.option: Turn off your microphone permanently to avoid background noise
Set your status to 'Busy' so no one knows you are on a call
Use a phone call instead — video is not professional

questions.question: What does 'Screen Share' do in Zoom or Teams?

options.option: Shows your computer screen to all meeting participants
options.comment: Screen sharing is used to present documents, slides, or demonstrate software to attendees.

options.option: Sends a copy of your screen as an email attachment
Takes a screenshot and saves it to Google Drive
Blocks other participants from seeing your video

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Mute' button during a video call?

options.option: To silence your microphone so others cannot hear your background noise
options.comment: Muting when not speaking is good meeting etiquette — it reduces distracting background sounds.

options.option: To turn off your camera so others cannot see you
To leave the meeting without notifying others
To record the meeting secretly

questions.question: What does it mean when a Zoom meeting has a 'Waiting Room' enabled?

options.option: Participants must wait for the host to admit them before joining
options.comment: The Waiting Room prevents uninvited attendees from joining automatically — a security feature.

options.option: The meeting has not started yet and will begin automatically
Your internet connection is too slow to join the meeting
You need to install a software update before joining

questions.question: What is a virtual background in a video call?

options.option: A digital image placed behind you to hide your actual surroundings
options.comment: Virtual backgrounds maintain privacy and professionalism when working from home.

options.option: A setting that records the call and sends it as background audio
A screen that shows all meeting participants at once
An automatic transcript of what everyone says

questions.question: What is Slack primarily used for?

options.option: Team messaging and collaboration through channels and direct messages
options.comment: Slack organises team communication into channels (topics) and direct messages for quick conversations.

options.option: Video calls only — it replaces Zoom for meetings
Sending formatted email newsletters to customers
Managing databases and project files

questions.question: What is an appropriate rule for professional online messaging?

options.option: Use clear, polite language and reply within a reasonable time
options.comment: Professional digital communication: no all-caps (shouting), clear subject, timely response.

options.option: Use as many abbreviations as possible to save time
Write in all capital letters to show emphasis
Never use punctuation — it slows down typing

questions.question: When is email a better choice than instant messaging?

options.option: For formal, detailed communication that needs a written record
options.comment: Email is best for formal requests, decisions, or anything that needs to be documented.

options.option: For quick back-and-forth conversations in real time
When you need to discuss something in under 30 seconds
When chatting with friends outside work

questions.question: What does '@mentioning' someone in a Slack message do?

options.option: Sends them a notification that they were specifically mentioned
options.comment: @ mentions ensure the right person is alerted. @channel notifies everyone in a channel.

options.option: Sends them an email copy of the message automatically
Creates a video call with that person immediately
Adds them as a co-worker in your contacts list

questions.question: WhatsApp Web allows you to:

options.option: Use WhatsApp from your computer browser linked to your phone
options.comment: WhatsApp Web mirrors your phone's WhatsApp — convenient for typing longer messages on a keyboard.

options.option: Create a WhatsApp account without a phone number
Send messages that automatically delete after 10 seconds
Make international calls for free without WiFi

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Slack 'channel'?

options.option: A shared space for a specific team or topic to keep conversations organised
options.comment: Channels (e.g. #marketing, #general) keep conversations sorted so messages reach the right people.

options.option: A private video call between two people
A folder for storing shared documents
A notification setting that mutes all messages

questions.question: What is a phishing email?

options.option: A fake email designed to trick you into revealing personal information
options.comment: Phishing emails impersonate trusted organisations to steal passwords or financial details.

options.option: An email that contains a large file attachment
A marketing newsletter you signed up for
An email that arrives in your spam folder by mistake

questions.question: Which of the following is a strong password?

options.option: Tr0ub4dor&3#Mn!
options.comment: Strong passwords mix uppercase, lowercase, digits, and symbols, and are at least 12 characters.

options.option: password123
your date of birth
your name followed by 1

questions.question: What is Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)?

options.option: A security step requiring a password plus a second verification (e.g. a code)
options.comment: 2FA protects accounts even if your password is stolen — the attacker still needs the second factor.

options.option: Using two different passwords for the same account
Signing in twice within 24 hours to stay active
Sharing your account with two authorised users

questions.question: What does a padlock icon in the browser address bar indicate?

options.option: The connection to the website is encrypted (HTTPS)
options.comment: A padlock means data is encrypted in transit. Always check for it before entering sensitive data.

options.option: The website is government-approved and verified
Your password for this site is saved in the browser
The website cannot be accessed by others while you browse

questions.question: Which is the safest practice when using public WiFi?

options.option: Avoid logging into banking or sensitive accounts on public WiFi
options.comment: Public WiFi is often unencrypted. Use a VPN or mobile data for sensitive tasks.

options.option: Always log into your bank to verify it works everywhere
Public WiFi is as safe as home WiFi since all routers are identical
Turn off HTTPS in browser settings when on public WiFi

questions.question: What should you do before clicking a link in a suspicious email?

options.option: Hover over the link to check the actual destination URL before clicking
options.comment: Hover preview reveals the true URL. If it looks unfamiliar or misspelled, do not click it.

options.option: Forward the email to all contacts to warn them first
Click quickly so your antivirus can scan it
Copy the link and post it in a search engine to test it

questions.question: Which platform is designed specifically for professional networking?

options.option: LinkedIn
options.comment: LinkedIn is the leading professional network for jobs, career development, and industry connections.

options.option: Instagram
TikTok
Snapchat

questions.question: What should a professional LinkedIn profile include?

options.option: A professional photo, clear headline, work experience, and skills
options.comment: A complete profile increases visibility to recruiters and establishes your professional identity.

options.option: Personal holiday photos and hobbies only
Your home address and phone number publicly visible
A list of complaints about previous employers

questions.question: What privacy setting should you check on social media accounts?

options.option: Who can see your posts, location, and personal details
options.comment: Regularly reviewing privacy settings limits unwanted data sharing and protects your personal information.

options.option: Whether your account is older than 5 years
The number of followers you have compared to others
The colour theme of your profile page

questions.question: Which behaviour is appropriate on a professional LinkedIn page?

options.option: Sharing industry news, skills updates, and engaging respectfully with others
options.comment: Professional content builds your personal brand. Casual posts are better suited to personal platforms.

options.option: Posting complaints about your current employer publicly
Using slang and emoji-heavy language in all messages
Sending bulk connection requests to thousands of strangers daily

questions.question: What does 'digital footprint' mean?

options.option: The trail of data you leave behind through your online activity
options.comment: Your digital footprint includes search history, social media posts, emails, and purchases — it can be seen by others.

options.option: The physical size of your computer monitor
How many apps are installed on your device
The amount of data stored on your USB drive

questions.question: Why should you think before posting personal information publicly online?

options.option: It can damage your reputation, compromise privacy, or be misused
options.comment: Online posts can be seen by employers, strangers, or scammers — and can be very difficult to remove.

options.option: Online posts expire after 24 hours and have no lasting impact
Your ISP automatically removes harmful posts on your behalf
Only posts with photos can be seen by others online

questions.question: What file format is most commonly used to share documents that should not be easily edited?

options.option: PDF (Portable Document Format)
options.comment: PDFs preserve formatting across devices and are difficult to edit, making them ideal for official documents.

options.option: .docx (Microsoft Word)
.xlsx (Microsoft Excel)
.txt (Plain Text)

questions.question: What is an electronic signature (e-signature)?

options.option: A legally recognised digital mark used to sign documents online
options.comment: E-signatures (e.g. DocuSign, Adobe Sign) are legally binding in most countries.

options.option: A scanned image of your handwritten signature pasted into a document
Your name typed in italic font at the bottom of an email
A PIN code used to access an online government portal

questions.question: Which tool can you use to fill and submit forms on government websites?

options.option: A web browser on any internet-connected device
options.comment: Most government e-services are web-based — no special software required, just a browser.

options.option: A USB printer connected to the government office's computer
Only a government-issued laptop with special software
A fax machine for secure transmission

questions.question: How do you convert a Microsoft Word document to a PDF?

options.option: File > Save As and select PDF from the file type dropdown
options.comment: Word (and most Office apps) export directly to PDF via File > Save As or File > Export.

options.option: Copy the text and paste it into Adobe Acrobat's blank page
Take a screenshot of each page and save as images
Open the file in Notepad and rename it with a .pdf extension

questions.question: When submitting an online form, what should you do before clicking 'Submit'?

options.option: Review all fields for accuracy and ensure required fields are complete
options.comment: Mistakes on submitted forms may be hard to correct and could delay applications or payments.

options.option: Clear your browser history so the form is submitted anonymously
Print the page before submitting it so you have a reference
Refresh the page to check the form loads correctly before submitting

questions.question: A mandatory field on an online form is indicated by:

options.option: An asterisk (*) beside the field label
options.comment: An asterisk (*) is the standard convention for required fields. The form won't submit without them.

options.option: A red background colour behind the field
The word 'Optional' in brackets next to the label
A double border around the input box

questions.question: What is a digital calendar primarily used for?

options.option: Scheduling appointments, meetings, and reminders
options.comment: Google Calendar and Outlook Calendar sync across devices and can send automatic reminders.

options.option: Storing files and documents in date order
Tracking website traffic and page visits
Recording your work hours for payroll automatically

questions.question: Which app is best for taking quick notes and organising them on your phone or computer?

options.option: Google Keep or Microsoft OneNote
options.comment: Note-taking apps sync across devices and support labels, reminders, and sharing.

options.option: Google Sheets
Microsoft PowerPoint
Adobe Photoshop

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using a task management tool like Microsoft To-Do or Google Tasks?

options.option: Track tasks, set due dates, and receive reminders to stay organised
options.comment: Task tools prevent things falling through the cracks by keeping all actions in one place.

options.option: Automatically complete your work tasks on your behalf
Block distracting websites while you work
Send emails automatically when tasks are due

questions.question: What is Google Workspace (formerly G Suite)?

options.option: A set of cloud-based productivity tools including Docs, Sheets, Drive, and Meet
options.comment: Google Workspace is Google's business productivity suite — cloud-based and collaborative by design.

options.option: A Windows application for managing local files
An antivirus solution provided by Google
Google's operating system for Chromebook only

questions.question: What does it mean to 'sync' an app across devices?

options.option: Your data (notes, calendar, files) updates automatically on all your devices
options.comment: Sync means any change you make on your phone appears on your laptop and vice versa, via the cloud.

options.option: The app is installed on all devices at the same time
Two devices are physically connected with a cable
Your battery levels are equalised between devices

questions.question: What is the purpose of setting a 'due date' on a task in a productivity app?

options.option: To receive a reminder and ensure the task is completed on time
options.comment: Due dates trigger alerts and help prioritise your workload — a core time management technique.

options.option: To automatically send the completed task to your manager
To lock the task so others cannot edit it
To bill the time to a client's invoice
